title: dombeya montana subsp. macrantha
intro: Dombeya montana subsp. macrantha, also known as the pink wild pear, is a species of flowering plant in the Sterculiaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Dombeya montana subsp. macrantha include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
